summary: Gruber dismantles the hype around Google and Levi's Jacquard smart jacket, arguing it's little more than a Bluetooth remote control holstered in a sleeve. He takes issue with a Mashable reviewer's claim that smart clothing represents the future of wearables over smartwatches. Gruber points out the jacket requires headphones and a phone to function, offers no fitness tracking, and only controls audio playback and Google Assistant. He argues Apple Watch is superior because it provides haptic feedback, a display when needed, and works in all conditions year-round. He further notes that AirPods already solve the screenless wearable problem better since they work with any outfit. The piece concludes that while displayless wearables have a future, this jacket isn't part of it.
key_points: The jacket is essentially a Bluetooth remote control housed in a custom sleeve, not truly embedded wearable technology
Without headphones and a phone, the jacket does nothing beyond keeping you warm — it's no smarter than standard earbud clickers
Smartwatches offer fitness tracking, notifications, haptic feedback, and an optional display — none of which the jacket provides
A jacket is weather-dependent and removed indoors, making it fundamentally worse as a wearable platform than a watch worn all day
AirPods already deliver screenless smart wearable functionality that works with every outfit and activity
money_quotes: It's not a smart jacket so much as a jacket designed with a custom sleeve to hold the smart device.
I'm hard pressed to come up with a more generous description of it than 'a Bluetooth remote control'.
One can certainly argue that smartwatches were overhyped, but it's preposterous to argue they're not useful at all.
Unless I'm missing something, if you don't have headphones in and your phone with you, this jacket does nothing other than keep you warm.
It's no smarter than the little clicker on the earbuds that come with every phone.
You can wear a watch every day, in all weather conditions. And you keep it on all day. A jacket is something you wear in specific weather conditions and generally take off once you get inside.
There is a future for wearable devices that don't have displays. AirPods are part of that future; a jacket with a Bluetooth remote in the sleeve, not so much.
key_insight: A smart jacket that requires headphones and a phone to function is just a glorified Bluetooth remote, solving a problem that AirPods and Apple Watch already handle better in a form factor you actually wear all day.
